mnemonics: SR Flip-Flop: Set and Reset (S=1 sets Q=1, R=1 resets Q=0)
JK Flip-Flop: Jump and Keep (J=1 jumps to 1, K=1 keeps at 0 or toggles)
D Flip-Flop: Data (Q follows D on clock edge)
T Flip-Flop: Toggle (T=1 toggles Q on clock edge)
keyPoints: Flip-flops are bistable multivibrators used as 1-bit storage elements.
They are edge-triggered (respond to clock transition) or level-triggered.
JK Flip-Flop: No invalid state; toggles when J=K=1.
D Flip-Flop: Simplest; stores the value of D.
T Flip-Flop: Toggles output when T=1.
Flip-flops are building blocks for registers and counters.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the invalid state in SR flip-flop (S=R=1).
Misunderstanding the toggle operation in JK and T flip-flops.
Not considering setup and hold times for reliable operation.
Using asynchronous inputs (like preset and clear) incorrectly.
